So for the kick-off, I will be assigning one owner to each work stream, so ownership is clear from day one.
Good. And what about the dependency between contracts and data?
This week I will be chasing legal daily so contracts close on time. We will not be starting data until that is done.
Makes sense. Will you be ramping up all four suppliers at once?
No, one at a time. By the end of week two we will have delivered the charter and the stakeholder map, and then I will be onboarding the first supplier.
Perfect. And keep an eye on scope creep -- if a new request lands, it comes to me first.

Morning, everyone, and welcome to the kick-off for the supplier-integration project. Before we dive in, let me set the direction. Over the next three months, we will be onboarding four new suppliers into the Bloomberg platform, and I will be running the kick-off cadence personally for the first few weeks. Here is how the work breaks down. Each work stream has a single owner, so ownership is clear from day one. The data stream depends on the contracts stream, so watch that dependency closely; if contracts slip, data slips too. We will be ramping up the pilot gradually, not all at once. While we ramp up, I will be tracking three success criteria: on-time onboarding, clean data, and zero disruption to the live platform. The deliverables for phase one are the project charter and the stakeholder map, both due by the end of week two. One more thing: guard against scope creep. If a new request comes in, it goes through me, not straight into a work stream. This time next month, we will be running two suppliers in parallel, so let us start strong and stay aligned.

Hi Marcus, this is Fabiana with a quick update after the kick-off. It went well. The charter and the stakeholder map are both signed off, so the deliverables for phase one are done ahead of schedule. I have confirmed the owner for each work stream, so ownership is clear across the board. There is one dependency I want to flag: the data stream cannot start until the contracts stream closes, and contracts are running a few days behind. So this coming week, I will be chasing the legal team daily to keep that on track. We will be onboarding the first supplier on Monday, and I will be sitting in on the cadence call to make sure the success criteria are clear to everyone. I am also watching for scope creep; two extra requests already came in, and I have parked both until phase two. By this time next week, we will be ramping up the second supplier. I will send the full charter tonight. Thanks, and talk soon.

Marcus opens the kick-off and sets the direction for the supplier-integration project. He assigns a single owner to each work stream so ownership is clear. He flags the dependency between the contracts stream and the data stream. The team agrees to ramp up the pilot gradually while tracking the success criteria. Fabiana confirms that the charter and the stakeholder map are signed off and parks two extra requests to avoid scope creep.
